title: i11 cmd hardening — atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn PRESENCE → VALUE+DOMAIN+GRADE anchor
fixpoint_marker: i11-hardened sealed — 5/5 candidates round-trip OK (ANCHOR_INTACT sentinels), --legacy-cmd backward-compat preserved, raw 71 SUGGEST mode unchanged
backfill_note: backfilled 2026-04-26 to satisfy registry schema (raw 77 additive) — trawl_id from filename, axes_surfaced=len(axes), fixpoint_marker derived from axis_5_verification evidence

axes.claim: PRESENCE-only template is vacuous against the silent-drift threat model
axes.evidence: F13_F22_candidate_review.md verdict: all 10 auto-spawn cmds use `^@<TYPE> <id> =` only; grep PASSes even after value drift (e.g. atlas changes `@P n = 6` to `@P n = 7` — falsifier still PASS, no HIT). Witness: F19 `mu` had to be hand-tightened to `^@P mu = mobius\(6\) = 1 :: foundation \[10\*\]` before merge.

Source line is already parsed for expr (sed `s/^@[PCFLRSXMTE] [^ ]+ = (.+) :: .*$/\1/p`) and domain (sed `.* :: ([^ ]+) \[.*$/\1/p`); grade extraction already in place. Adding a regex-escape pass on expr+grade gives full literal anchor with zero new state. Sentinel rename `PRESENT_<ID>_<TYPE>` → `<ID_UPPER>_ANCHOR_INTACT` matches the 4 hand-merged F19-F22 entries already in registry.
axes.witness: tool/atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh lines 141-157 + design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl lines 13-16 (F19-F22 reference shape)

axes.claim: sed bracket expression `[][(){}.*+?^$|\\]` covers all BRE/ERE metacharacters seen in atlas exprs/grades; bash-3.2 / macOS sed compatible
axes.evidence: Test cases: `mobius(6) = 1` → `mobius\(6\) = 1`; `10*` → `10\*`; `sigma^2 = 144` → `sigma\^2 = 144` (verified live). JSON-emission second-pass replaces `\` → `\\` so decoded grep pattern stays valid. 5/5 emitted hardened cmds round-trip through `bash -c` to expected sentinel.
axes.witness: live shell verification — all 5 candidates emit ANCHOR_INTACT sentinel

axes.claim: --legacy-cmd flag preserves pre-hardened PRESENCE-only template for diagnostic / replay against pre-2026-04-26 candidate baselines
axes.evidence: Default branch (no flag) → hardened cmd + ANCHOR_INTACT sentinel. `--legacy-cmd` branch → original `^@<TYPE> <id> =` cmd + `PRESENT_<ID>_<TYPE>` sentinel. Sentinel-line includes `mode=hardened|legacy` tag for log-grep visibility. raw 71 unchanged: both modes write to state/falsifier_candidates.jsonl, neither auto-merges into design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl.
axes.witness: tool/atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh lines 32-69 (CLI parse + help) + 142-155 (mode branch) + 174-179 (sentinel mode tag)

axes.claim: Hardened cmds pass round-trip — JSON-decode → bash -c → expected sentinel, for all 5 default candidates
axes.evidence: Run 1: `> state/falsifier_candidates.jsonl && bash tool/atlas_falsifier_auto_spawn.sh --emit-jsonl --limit 5` → 5 candidates. python3 json.loads + subprocess.run for each: F18→N_ANCHOR_INTACT, F19→SIGMA_ANCHOR_INTACT, F20→PHI_ANCHOR_INTACT, F21→TAU_ANCHOR_INTACT, F22→SOPFR_ANCHOR_INTACT. All [OK]. Run 2: --legacy-cmd → 5 candidates with PRESENT_<ID>_<TYPE> sentinels (manual inspection confirms PRESENCE-only template).
axes.witness: state/falsifier_candidates.jsonl (post-run, hardened mode) + scripted bash -c round-trip 5/5 OK

scope_note: raw 71 SUGGEST mode preserved — script does NOT mutate design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl. Manual merge path unchanged: `cat state/falsifier_candidates.jsonl >> design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl` after human review.
non_goals: hexa runtime dependency (self-host avoid — bash + grep + sed only)
rewrite of already-merged F19-F22 (those were hand-tightened with the same template; no regression)
follow_ups: F13/F14/F15/F16/F17 still in retired/duplicate state per F13_F22 review — no action; F18 (n) now naturally re-spawned with hardened cmd, supersedes legacy F13 (auto-rejected as F12 covers it stronger via triple-source)
consider --replay-legacy mode to diff hardened vs legacy candidate output across atlas snapshots (cross-snapshot drift detector)
